2606.23403.title: Litmus: Zero-Label, Code-Driven Metric Specification for Evaluating AI Systems
2606.23403.paper_summary: As agentic LLM systems move from prototypes to deployment across increasingly diverse domains, evaluating them has become both more important and more difficult. The challenge is not only that individual metrics may be unreliable, but that evaluation goals are often left implicit. Without a clear account of what a system is expected to do, how it can fail, and which failures matter, metric choices become difficult to justify, interpret, or validate. We present Litmus, a zero-label system that designs evaluation and monitoring metrics for AI pipelines by eliciting evaluation intent from source code and targeted interrogation. Instead of assuming that the evaluation target is already known, Litmus first identifies what must be measured and why, then converts those answers into constraints for constructing a justified, per-stage metric portfolio. We evaluate Litmus on three real, code-defined AI pipelines - financial account grouping, scientific QA, and inherent risk assessment - against AutoMetrics and three DynamicRubric baselines. Litmus achieves the broadest or tied-broadest concern coverage, spans more pipeline stages, produces a near-zero-redundancy portfolio, and ranks first in validity against per-row quality labels on all three pipelines - decisively on scientific QA (Spearman $ρ=0.72$ vs. less than $0.47$ for every baseline), and within overlapping confidence intervals in relation to two components of the audit framework despite using no labels during metric design. Our results support a shift from automatic metric implementation to automatic metric specification: before asking which metric to compute, evaluation systems should ask what must be measured and why.
2606.23403.paper_summary_zh: 隨著具有主動性的 LLM 系統從原型轉向在越來越多樣化的領域中部署，對它們的評估變得越來越重要且困難。挑戰不僅在於個別指標可能不可靠，還在於評估目標通常是隱含的。沒有明確的系統預期行為、可能的失敗方式及其重要性的說明，指標選擇就變得難以辯護、解釋或驗證。我們提出了 Litmus，一個零標籤系統，通過從源代碼和針對性詢問中引出評估意圖，設計 AI 管道的評估和監控指標。Litmus 首先確定必須測量的內容及其原因，而不是假設評估目標已知，然後將這些答案轉換為構建合理的、每個階段的指標組合的約束。我們對 Litmus 在三個真實的、代碼定義的 AI 管道上進行評估——金融賬戶分組、科學問答和內在風險評估——並與 AutoMetrics 和三個 DynamicRubric 基準進行比較。Litmus 在關注範圍上達到最廣或並列最廣，涵蓋更多管道階段，產生近乎零冗餘的組合，並在所有三個管道上對每行質量標籤的有效性排名第一——在科學問答上具有決定性（Spearman $ρ=0.72$，而每個基準均低於 $0.47$），並在與審計框架的兩個組件相關的重疊置信區間內，即使在指標設計過程中未使用任何標籤。我們的結果支持從自動指標實施轉向自動指標規範的轉變：在詢問計算哪個指標之前，評估系統應該先詢問必須測量什麼及其原因。
2606.23403.author: Prajjwal Gupta et.al.
2606.23403.authors: Prajjwal Gupta, Prasang Gupta, Vishal Bhutani, Apoorva Sharma, Sumanth Chundru, Waqar Sarguroh, Kevin Paul
